items.title: ZCode – Harness for GLM-5.2
items.summary: ZCode为GLM-5.2提供测试框架，核心是自动化评估和调优大模型性能，值得关注因其提升模型部署可靠性。

items.title: Senior SWE-Bench: open-source benchmark that assesses agents as senior engineers
items.summary: Senior SWE-Bench是开源基准测试，模拟高级工程师场景评估AI代理，创新在于复杂任务设计，推动AI工程能力评估。

items.title: Oomwoo, an open-source robot vacuum you build yourself
items.summary: Oomwoo是开源DIY机器人吸尘器，用户可自行组装，核心技术是模块化硬件和开源固件，适合创客和智能家居爱好者。

items.title: Global review confirms mRNA vaccines are safe, effective and full of promise
items.summary: 全球综述确认mRNA疫苗安全有效且前景广阔，基于大规模数据验证，为未来疫苗开发提供坚实证据。

items.title: Bring back crappy forums
items.summary: 呼吁回归简单论坛形式，反对现代社交平台复杂性，强调社区互动和内容质量，值得关注其反主流设计理念。

items.title: Opening up 'Zero-Knowledge Proof' technology to promote privacy in age assurance

items.title: FFmpeg 9.1's new AAC encoder
items.summary: FFmpeg 9.1新AAC编码器提升音频压缩效率和质量，采用先进算法，适合音视频处理优化。

items.title: Measuring the Gap Between Human and LLM Research Ideas
items.description: LLMs are increasingly used to brainstorm research ideas, but existing evaluations mostly judge individual ideas by novelty, feasibility, or expert preference. We instead ask: how far are current LLM-generated ideas from human researchers? To characterize this gap, we build a large-scale evaluation framework for ideation from high-quality human research papers. For each paper, we reverse-engineer a small set of closely related prior works that likely inspired its core idea. LLMs are then prompted
items.summary: 衡量人类与LLM研究想法之间的差距，构建大规模评估框架来量化差异

items.title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
items.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a central component of post-training large language models (LLMs), yet little is understood about how RL adaptation is distributed across transformer layers. Existing approaches typically update all model parameters uniformly, implicitly assuming that every layer contributes similarly to the gains obtained during RL post-training. In this work, we challenge this assumption through a systematic layer-wise study of RL training. Surprisingly, we find that trai
items.summary: 仅训练单层Transformer即可匹配全参数强化学习的效果，揭示RL适应在层间的分布特性

items.title: Language-Critique Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Demonstrations
items.description: Prior work on imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations typically relies on compressed supervision signals such as confidence estimates, discriminator scores, or importance weights. These scalar signals are inherently limited, as they cannot explicitly express intermediate reasoning about task progress, failure modes, or corrective actions. We propose a language-critique framework for imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations that instead leverages natural language as a structure
items.summary: 通过语言批评从次优演示中进行模仿学习，用显式推理信号替代标量监督

items.title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill
items.description: Memory expertise is a learned skill: knowing what to encode, when to retrieve, and how to organize knowledge--a capacity known in cognitive science as metamemory. We bring this perspective to LLMs by treating memory management as a trainable skill. We promote file-system operations to first-class memory actions alongside task actions, letting the model itself decide how to manage its memory. This memory skill improves along two axes: the structure that supports it (prompts, file schemas, action
items.summary: 将记忆管理作为可训练技能，通过文件系统操作实现LLM的元记忆能力

items.title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
items.description: When should an AI system's answer be trusted? Formal proof assistants offer certainty but cannot reach most of the problem distribution; scalar LLM judges offer coverage but produce opaque scores that cannot be audited after the fact and are subject to the same coherence issues as any LLM. We present Theoria, a verification architecture that closes this gap. A candidate solution is rewritten into a sequence of typed state transitions, each licensed by an explicit justification, whether that be a
items.summary: 提出重写可接受性验证框架，在非正式推理状态下实现可审计的AI可信度判断

items.title: Ink3D: Sculpting 3D Assets with Extremely Complex Textures via Video Generative Models
items.description: Recent 3D generative models can synthesize high-quality geometry but often struggle to reproduce intricate textures from reference images, largely due to the scarcity of large-scale 3D training data with rich surface appearance. In contrast, visual generative models are trained on datasets several orders of magnitude larger and excel at modeling complex visual patterns. Motivated by this gap, we introduce Ink3D, a framework that bridges 3D generation with large-scale video generative models to s
items.summary: 利用视频生成模型从参考图像中合成具有复杂纹理的3D资产，解决训练数据稀缺问题

items.title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
items.description: Transformers use the same forward computation stream to both predict the next token and store useful state for future token predictions. We formulate the \emph{state-prediction separation hypothesis}: disentangling the two roles yields better language modeling performance. We design a Transformer variant that uses two computation streams to separate the two functions, and conduct pretraining experiments across various scales. Our experiments show that state-prediction separation consistently off
items.summary: 提出状态-预测分离假说，通过解耦Transformer的两种角色提升语言建模性能

items.title: RepoRescue: An Empirical Study of LLM Agents on Whole-Repository Compatibility Rescue
items.description: Open-source libraries and tools are widely reused, but compatibility maintenance is expensive. Once maintainers leave, useful repositories can stop working as runtimes and dependencies evolve. We study whether LLM agents can adapt old repositories to modern environments, a task we call compatibility rescue. Unlike bug repair, compatibility rescue starts from a repository that worked in its original environment but fails after ecosystem drift. RepoRescue gives agents only the repository and its f
items.summary: 研究LLM代理对旧仓库进行全仓库兼容性修复的能力，应对依赖演化问题

items.title: FurnitureVLA: Learning Long-Horizon Bimanual Furniture Assembly with Vision-Language-Action Model
items.description: Current work on robot furniture assembly mostly focuses on toy-scale settings or single-arm manipulation. We introduce FurnitureVLA, the first systematic study of real-scale bimanual furniture assembly using Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs). We formalize the task, develop a scalable simulation pipeline for expert data generation and evaluation, and build a VR teleoperation system for single-operator bimanual control to collect high-quality real-world demonstrations. To address extreme long-h
items.summary: 提出FurnitureVLA，首个使用视觉-语言-动作模型实现真实尺寸双臂家具组装的工作

items.title: Are Performance-Optimization Benchmarks Reliably Measuring Coding Agents?
items.description: Repository-level performance-optimization benchmarks such as GSO, SWE-Perf and SWE-fficiency evaluate coding agents by applying patches to real repositories and comparing runtime against unoptimized baselines and official reference patches. Their leaderboard scores are increasingly used as evidence of coding-agent progress, but those scores can conflate runtime instability, benchmark-specific scoring rules, and how many tasks are already solved by at least one public submission. We audit these i
items.summary: 质疑性能优化基准对编码代理评估的可靠性，揭示基准设计中的潜在偏差
